key_suppliers: TP Oil & Gas Mexico, S. De R.L. De C.V.
dependencies: Third-party natural gas pipelines for transportation
Interstate pipeline systems (El Paso Natural Gas, Transwestern Pipeline, Kern River Pipeline Company, Mojave Pipeline Company)
Electric transmission lines owned by third parties
LNG supply from Tangguh PSC through 2029
Natural gas supply from southwestern U.S. suppliers
Equipment, materials, commodities and labor subject to supply chain constraints
risks: Failure to reach positive final investment decisions
Cost increases due to inflationary pressures, tariffs
Counterparty performance risks under contractual arrangements
Failure to obtain, modify, and/or maintain permits and regulatory approvals
Construction delays and cost overruns on LNG projects
Failure to negotiate, complete and maintain suitable commercial agreements
Shortages of or delays in obtaining equipment, materials, commodities or labor
Concentration risk with single EPC contractors for major projects
Failure to secure binding customer commitments for projects in development
Inability to obtain sufficient financing for projects
Supply chain disruptions affecting construction materials and labor availability
Disruptions from failures in transmission grid or pipeline systems
Dependence on third-party transportation facilities for natural gas and electricity delivery
Reliance on third-party vendors and contractors for construction and maintenance
Supply chain and gas transportation capacity constraints
Inflationary pressures on labor, materials and supplies costs

FERC rate matters and transmission revenue requirement determinations for SDG&E

DOE authorization requirements for LNG export to non-FTA countries

SEC climate-related disclosure rules
Texas Reliability Entity reliability standards enforcement
California SB 100 renewable energy mandates (50% by 2026, 60% by 2030, 90% by 2035, 95% by 2040)
Changes to Mexican electricity market regulations limiting private renewable energy
PUCT regulatory oversight of Oncor and Sharyland Utilities in Texas
DOT/PHMSA pipeline safety regulations
Mexican government amendments to Hydrocarbons Law and Electricity Industry Law
Environmental impact authorization challenges in Mexico for ECA facilities
ASEA (Mexico's National Agency for Industrial Safety and Environmental Protection) regulation
Rate recovery disallowances for costs deemed imprudent
TCEQ air quality permits for Port Arthur LNG projects subject to litigation
California AB 1279 net-zero GHG emissions by 2045 requirement
CPUC affiliate transaction rules
PUCT (Public Utility Commission of Texas) rate regulation
NRC licensing and oversight for SONGS decommissioning
Natural gas demand reduction policies in California
Mexican Federal Anticorruption Law compliance
Rate recovery mechanisms subject to CPUC approval and potential disallowances
Hazardous waste, air and water quality, land use regulations
Labor relations and collective bargaining agreement negotiations
U.S. Foreign Corrupt Practices Act compliance
Mexican Constitutional energy reforms transferring powers from CRE to SENER
Wildfire Legislation (AB 1054) and Wildfire Fund requirements
DOE (U.S. Department of Energy) LNG export approval requirements
California AB 32 (Global Warming Solutions Act) compliance
Potential revocation of self-supply permits in Mexico deemed improperly obtained
ERCOT market structure and reliability standards
Mexican Hydrocarbons Law permit suspension/revocation powers
Pipeline safety regulations and integrity management requirements
Credit rating agency actions affecting financing costs and conditions
Regulatory lag in recovering increased costs due to inflation
Cost of capital adjustment mechanism (CCM) triggers
Wildfire mitigation cost recovery and reasonableness review by CPUC
OEIS (Office of Energy Infrastructure Safety) wildfire mitigation plan approval
Aliso Canyon storage facility operational restrictions and potential closure
Franchise agreement renewals and municipalization risks
Franchise agreement requirements with municipalities
California enhanced state privacy laws
Federal and state environmental laws requiring investigation and remediation
NRC (Nuclear Regulatory Commission) oversight of SONGS decommissioning
CRE regulatory oversight in Mexico for natural gas and electricity operations
CARB GHG emission allowance requirements and compliance costs
CPUC citation and enforcement programs for safety violations
General Rate Case (GRC) outcomes and cost recovery limitations
EPA environmental compliance requirements
CPUC regulatory proceedings and rate-setting decisions affecting SDG&E and SoCalGas
Potential changes to Mexican Constitution affecting energy sector operations
Cost of capital proceedings and authorized return on equity determinations
CRE (Comisión Reguladora de Energía) regulation in Mexico
CPUC (California Public Utilities Commission) regulation of rates, capital structure, depreciation, resource procurement
FERC transmission rate proceedings and ROE determinations
FERC (Federal Energy Regulatory Commission) regulation of interstate natural gas sales, electric transmission, wholesale electricity sales

sanction_risks: Foreign policy positions and sanctions affecting international business
Trade policy shifts affecting imports/exports between U.S. and Mexico
Mexican retaliatory tariffs in response to U.S. tariffs
U.S. tariffs on Mexico proposed by current Administration (deferred but may become effective)
U.S. tariffs on steel and aluminum imports beginning March 12, 2025
